# How Bioprocess Monitoring Improves Quality Control in Manufacturing

![Blog Bioprocess 2](https://superiorsensors.com/wp-content/uploads/Blog-Bioprocess-2.webp)

Bioprocess manufacturing hinges on precise pressure control, especially in systems like bioreactors, where even small deviations can disrupt cell growth and nutrient transfer. Any lapse can lead to drops in productivity or compromised product quality. Advanced sensors, such as those developed by Superior Sensor Technology, help manufacturers maintain tight control over these variables, optimizing production and ensuring compliance with strict regulations. These innovations allow for consistent, high-quality results, keeping processes efficient and reliable.

## The Critical Role of Pressure Monitoring in Bioprocessing

Pressure control is fundamental to the success of bioreactor systems. Whether it’s regulating gas flow rates, ensuring the correct media exchange, or managing the conditions inside the bioreactor, pressure fluctuations can profoundly impact cell cultures and the biochemical environment. This is why high-performance pressure sensors are vital for real-time monitoring and control.

![An image describing what pressure sensors in bioprocessing can do](https://superiorsensors.com/wp-content/uploads/Pressure-Sensors-in-Bioprocessing-Image.png)

Superior Sensor Technology’s pressure sensors, specifically designed for bioprocess environments, offer several advantages:

These features make pressure sensors an indispensable component of quality control in bioprocess monitoring. They ensure the high throughput and reproducibility demanded by modern biomanufacturing.

## Pressure Sensors in Critical Bioprocess Applications

One critical application of pressure sensors in bioprocessing is regulating gas exchange. Dissolved oxygen (DO) levels, for instance, are closely tied to the pressure of gases (oxygen or air) supplied to the bioreactor. Maintaining precise pressure is critical for delivering the correct amount of oxygen, which directly influences cell growth and metabolite concentrations. Superior Sensor Technology’s pressure sensors ensure consistent gas flow by monitoring and adjusting the pressure in real time, preventing any oxygen deficits or excesses that could impair the bioprocess.

Another critical application is in controlling pressure within filtration systems. Maintaining steady pressure across filtration membranes ensures that the sterile filtration of bioreactor contents is efficient and reliable, protecting the purity and integrity of the product.

## Integration of Pressure Sensors into Process Control Systems

Pressure sensors are also vital components of comprehensive process control frameworks. These sensors provide real-time data that can be integrated into Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) and Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems. By feeding pressure data into these centralized control platforms, manufacturers can automate the adjustment of pressure-related parameters, reducing the risk of human error and improving overall system efficiency.

Additionally, integrating pressure sensors with process analytical technology (PAT) enables dynamic feedback loops that respond to changing conditions inside the bioreactor. For instance, if pressure sensors detect a drop in air supply pressure, the PAT system can automatically adjust air flow rates to restore optimal conditions, minimizing the risk of compromised batch quality.

## Pressure Sensors for Compliance and Quality Assurance

Ensuring compliance with regulatory standards such as those set by the FDA or EMA is a major concern in biomanufacturing. Pressure sensors are crucial in meeting these regulatory requirements, particularly in maintaining traceability and documenting process integrity. Calibration of pressure sensors is essential for ensuring their accuracy over time. Superior Sensor Technology’s solutions are designed to undergo rigorous validation protocols such as Installation Qualification (IQ) and Operational Qualification (OQ), providing confidence in the accuracy and reliability of pressure measurements.

By maintaining tight control over pressure levels, manufacturers can ensure that their processes remain within the specified parameters, thereby avoiding regulatory non-compliance and ensuring high product quality.

## Superior Sensor Technology: Pioneering Precision in Bioprocessing

Superior Sensor Technology offers cutting-edge pressure sensors tailored for bioprocessing’s complexities. Our sensors provide unmatched performance in monitoring and controlling pressure, enabling real-time adjustments that ensure consistent product quality. Designed with robustness, our sensors can withstand the rigors of sterilization cycles, resist biofouling, and maintain their accuracy over extended periods of use.

Our pressure sensors integrate seamlessly with your existing control systems, providing the real-time insights necessary to keep your bioprocesses running smoothly and within regulatory guidelines. With high sensitivity, fast response times, and long-term stability, Superior Sensor Technology is your trusted partner in optimizing bioprocess manufacturing for improved quality control.

Effective pressure monitoring is at the core of quality control in bioprocessing. By integrating advanced pressure sensors into bioreactor monitoring systems, manufacturers can maintain tight control over critical parameters like gas exchange, dissolved oxygen, and filtration pressure. Superior Sensor Technology offers robust, high-performance sensors that deliver precise, real-time pressure data, ensuring that bioprocesses are optimized for both productivity and quality.

# Sensors & Automation to Simplify Bioprocess Operations

[Download News](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/wp-content/uploads/faq-pdf/broadley-james-post-sensors__automation_to_simplify_bioprocess_operations.pdf)

Utilising sensors & automation to simplify bioprocess operations means that more work can be done in less time. By automating the routine tasks carried out in the lab, valuable time can be devoted to more important tasks.

Here are two practical examples of automating routine tasks in upstream and downstream bioprocessing with relatively simple instrumentation.

#### Automated Sampling

Scheduled automated sampling from a bioreactor.  This can be from a conventional glass benchtop vessel, rocker, SUB, or SIP stainless steel fermenter.   The sterile sample is delivered directly to a fraction collector for analysis at a later time, or directly to an analyser such as a nutrient monitor, cell counter or biochemistry analyser. There is also the possibility to use the analyser results to automatically adjust the feed rate of a pump for nutrient control.  These types of systems are set to run 24/7 meaning samples can be taken outside normal working hours.

![Flownamics 1200 SegFlow Auto Sampler with Nova & ViCell Analysers](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20125'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![Flownamics 1200 SegFlow Auto Sampler with Nova & ViCell Analysers](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/Seg-Flow_NovaViCell_Photo-300x125.jpg)

Flownamics 1200 SegFlow Auto Sampler

![Flownamics 1200 SegFlow Auto Sampler with Nova & ViCell Analysers](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/Seg-Flow_NovaViCell_Photo-300x125.jpg)

Decreased data variability (removing human error from sampling) by ensuring:

The Flownamics Seg-Flow® Automated On-line Sampling System provides multiple bioprocess monitoring and control solutions for improving process performance. The Seg-Flow system’s patented Segmented On-line Sampling™ technology allows rapid and accurate sampling for up to 8 vessels or process streams and sample delivery to 4 analyzers and/or fraction collectors. Several feed control options are available with the Seg-Flow system, which can control 2 pumps per vessel. Existing off-line and at-line analytics are seamlessly integrated into a multi-functional on-line PAT tool through the Seg-Flow system’s communication interface and FlowWeb™ control software. The FlowWeb software simultaneously acquires all integrated instrument data and can export the data to any OPC-enabled SCADA for enhanced process monitoring and control.

#### Automated TFF

Using feedback control from inline sensors means that a TFF (tangential flow filtration) purification system can be set and left to run to an end point, unattended.  This could be a conductivity set point or pH or even UV.  These sensors are small and low cost, so they can be used in single use systems and multi use systems, from bench scale through to production.

![PendoTECH TFF System](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20300%20155'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![PendoTECH TFF System](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/TFF-300x155.jpg)

PendoTECH TFF System

![PendoTECH TFF System](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/wp-content/uploads/2015/01/TFF-300x155.jpg)

The PendoTECH TFF Process Control System™ is an optimal solution that adds automation and data collection to a Cross Flow / Tangential Flow Filtration Process. It includes a powerful yet easy to use Graphical User Interface and recipe based control of entire concentration and diafiltration process.  Multiple options are available for automated diafiltration end point (filtrate weight, conductivity, pH, air in feed line with non-invasive detector, total flow). Pressure measurement and calculation of trans-membrane pressure (TMP) and delta-P with PendoTECH Pressure Sensors™.  Industry standard scales & pumps (peristaltic, rotary lobe, diaphragm, etc) can be interfaced with the system along with other optional accessories to create an advanced yet flexible system. It is ideal for process development and pilot plant where the project requirements can change.

#### Consistent Results

Incorporating sensors & automation into your process, also provides repeatability and consistency in results.  Data is collected digitally and can be distributed to corporate archiving and integration into SCADA systems.  Processes can be run to pre-defined menus, ensuring each step is recorded.  Reliable and simple operation provides a level of control so that the operator can “walk away” from the process with confidence.

Broadley-James Ltd distributes a wide range of sensors & automation equipment specifically designed for bioprocessing.  We have selected partners who are Industry Leaders in their field, more information is available on our website [here](https://www.broadleyjames.eu/bioprocess/partners/).

## Impacts

Improved capability to assess iPSC intracellular metabolic state in real time

Advanced understanding of iPSC metabolism in static and bioreactor cultures with aggregates and on microcarriers

Reliable TPE sensor developed with predictive power for iPSC pluripotency, growth rate, and productivity (CQAs)

Improved prediction and control of CQAs and outcomes for iPSC culture process using online sensing and KG-ML framework

enriched_points.expanded_content: Advanced sensors are crucial in bioprocess control, providing real-time monitoring of critical parameters like pressure, temperature, and pH, ensuring optimal conditions for cell growth and metabolic activity.
Control software has evolved to integrate seamlessly with sensors, enabling automated data analysis and decision-making, which reduces human error and enhances process efficiency in biomanufacturing.

Implementing advanced automation technologies significantly boosts operational efficiency, allowing for continuous monitoring and adjustments, leading to higher product quality and reduced downtime in bioprocessing.
